title: Quick Start Guide for Project Teams Adopting GenAI Solutions
subtitle: A practical playbook for EPAM teams delivering GenAI solutions — from POC to MVP

curriculum.title: Key Principles
curriculum.description: Six technology-agnostic principles covering expectation management, context engineering, data products, measurement, ROI framing, and incremental delivery.

children.title: Set realistic stakeholder expectations
children.description: Align users and sponsors around what LLMs can and cannot reliably do, and set acceptable quality targets.

children.title: Mastering Context Engineering
children.description: Curate what information the model sees — system prompts, metadata, ontologies, RAG, and GraphRAG — as the path to reliable AI results.

children.title: Data products matter even more
children.description: Garbage in, garbage out — curated, domain-oriented data products are the prerequisite for trustworthy GenAI outcomes.

children.title: You can't improve what you don't measure
children.description: Combine automated evaluation and real user feedback to drive continuous, measurable quality improvements.

children.title: Use ROI to discuss costs
children.description: Shift the conversation from raw spend to ROI — define personas, success metrics, and plan cost optimization as a deliberate phase.

children.title: Start small, grow complexity gradually
children.description: Begin with a proven data product and guided tooling. Add RAG, GraphRAG, and multi-agent orchestration only after baseline reliability.

curriculum.title: Evaluation Approach
curriculum.description: How to design and run evaluations for GenAI solutions — test case curation, automated benchmarks, user feedback, and regression tracking.
